meta.title: Process Transformation Accelerator — Navigation Spine
meta.tagline: From client mission to running agent — one continuous pipeline.
meta.note: Function-agnostic. Order-to-Cash is fully built; other functions are samples that prove the framework generalises.

stages.name: Design
stages.blurb: Pick the mission, choose the patterns, configure the action blocks.

stages.name: Discover & Fit
stages.blurb: Assess every action block for agent fit; record and agentivise the happy path.

stages.name: Run & Govern
stages.blurb: Straight-through on the happy path; variations matched to patterns and routed for approval.

functions.name: Order-to-Cash
functions.tagline: AR collections, disputes, credit risk — the fully-built core.

areas.name: Collections Mgmt
sub: Promise to pay / payment plan capture

processes.name: AR Collections & Dispute Management
processes.desc: Behavioural collections: read intent, sequence the right action, escalate only when warranted.

functions.name: Procure-to-Pay
functions.tagline: Invoice integrity, matching, and working-capital capture.

processes.name: AP Invoice Processing & Exceptions
processes.desc: From invoice receipt to posting — duplicates, mismatches, and spend leakage.

functions.name: Record-to-Report
functions.tagline: A faster, cleaner close — reconciliations, accruals, intercompany.

processes.name: Financial Close & Reconciliation
processes.desc: Period-end: clear breaks, post accruals, balance intercompany.

functions.name: Hire-to-Retire
functions.tagline: Onboarding that's ready on day one, compliantly.

processes.name: Employee Onboarding
processes.desc: From offer-accept to productive day-one — provisioning and compliance.

processes.name: Order Fulfillment & Replenishment
processes.desc: Keep shelves stocked and promises kept — stockouts and slippage.

functions.name: Financial Planning & Analysis
functions.tagline: Rolling forecast, variance insight, and decision support — the perform half of finance.

processes.name: Forecasting & Variance Management
processes.desc: Keep the forecast honest and the variances explained — bias out, insight in.

objectives.name: Keep the rolling forecast accurate & bias-free

functions.name: Treasury & Cash Management
functions.tagline: Cash visibility, liquidity, and FX / covenant risk — money in motion.

processes.name: Cash & Liquidity Management
processes.desc: See the cash, sweep the idle, and catch the risk before it's a surprise.

functions.name: Tax Management
functions.tagline: Determination, compliance, and provisioning — accurate, penalty-free tax.

processes.name: Indirect Tax Compliance & Filing
processes.desc: File on time and right — reconcile to the ledger, determine at source, never miss a deadline.

functions.name: Travel & Expense
functions.tagline: Expense capture, policy, and reimbursement — spend that behaves.

processes.name: Expense Audit & Compliance
processes.desc: Catch the leakage and keep reimbursement frictionless — audit smart, pay clean.

functions.name: Internal Controls & Compliance
functions.tagline: Continuous control monitoring and segregation of duties — trust, evidenced.

processes.name: Continuous Control Monitoring & SoD
processes.desc: Catch control failures and toxic access before they become losses — assurance, continuously.

2001.name: Duplicate Invoice Slip
2001.mentalModel: Same vendor, same amount, near-identical date — is this a re-bill or a genuine duplicate? Fingerprint it before it clears.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2001.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Duplicate Invoice Slip pattern.
2001.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2001.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Duplicate_Fingerprint
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Duplicate_Fingerprint'

2002.name: PO–Invoice 3-Way Mismatch
2002.mentalModel: Invoice says 1,000 units, GR says 980. Is this within tolerance, a short-ship, or a price creep? Decide the variance, don't bounce it to a human by default.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2002.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the PO–Invoice 3-Way Mismatch pattern.
2002.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2002.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for ThreeWay_Variance
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'ThreeWay_Variance'

2003.name: Maverick / Off-Contract Spend
2003.mentalModel: This buy bypassed the preferred-supplier contract. Was it an emergency, or leakage? Surface it before payment, attribute the savings lost.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2003.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Maverick / Off-Contract Spend pattern.
2003.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2003.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Off_Contract_Flag
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Off_Contract_Flag'

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF Off_Contract AND Amount < threshold

2004.name: Early-Payment Discount Capture
2004.mentalModel: There's a 2/10 net-30 discount on the table and the clock is ticking. Is the discount worth more than the cash held? Capture it automatically when it is.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2004.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Early-Payment Discount Capture pattern.
2004.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2004.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Discount_Window
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Discount_Window'

2005.name: Remit-To Bank Swap
2005.mentalModel: The bank details on this invoice don't match the vendor master. A changed remit-to is the number-one invoice-redirection fraud vector — I never pay to it until it's verified on a known-good phone number, no matter how routine the invoice looks.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2005.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Remit-To Bank Swap pattern.
2005.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2005.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for RemitTo_Change_Flag
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'RemitTo_Change_Flag'

2006.name: Resubmission-After-Reject
2006.mentalModel: This 'new' invoice number is last week's rejected invoice wearing a disguise — same vendor, PO, and amount. If the original gets corrected and this one clears too, we pay twice. I match on the economic fingerprint, not the invoice number.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2006.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Resubmission-After-Reject pattern.
2006.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2006.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Resubmit_Fingerprint
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Resubmit_Fingerprint'

branchingDAG.hitl: AP analyst confirms not a duplicate

hitlGates: AP analyst confirms not a duplicate

2007.name: Unapplied Credit Memo
2007.mentalModel: The vendor issued a credit memo weeks ago but keeps billing the full amount, and the credit is just sitting there unapplied. Every gross invoice I clear without netting it is money we've effectively paid twice. Net it before it posts.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2007.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Unapplied Credit Memo pattern.
2007.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2007.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Open_Credit_Balance
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Open_Credit_Balance'

2008.name: Round-Sum Non-PO Inflation
2008.mentalModel: A suspiciously round non-PO invoice — exactly $10,000, no line detail — from a vendor with barely any history. It's not proof of anything, but round-sum, low-history, no-backup is the classic soft-fraud signature. I want a second look before it slides into an auto-approve tier.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2008.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Round-Sum Non-PO Inflation pattern.
2008.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2008.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for RoundSum_Anomaly
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'RoundSum_Anomaly'

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF No_Backup AND Amount > threshold

2009.name: Threshold-Split Approval Dodge
2009.mentalModel: Three $9,900 invoices from the same vendor this week, each one just under the $10k approval gate. Individually every one is auto-approvable; together it's a $29,700 decision someone senior should actually see. I aggregate the window before I let any of them approve.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2009.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Threshold-Split Approval Dodge pattern.
2009.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2009.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Split_Under_Threshold
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Split_Under_Threshold'

2010.name: Approval Musical Chairs
2010.mentalModel: This invoice has bounced to a third approver in a week, each one saying 'not mine'. The delegation-of-authority is ambiguous and the invoice is aging while they pass it around. I pin the correct approver by amount and cost-centre authority and stop the merry-go-round.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2010.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Approval Musical Chairs pattern.
2010.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2010.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Approval_Bounce
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Approval_Bounce'

branchingDAG.condition: ELSE IF DoA_Ambiguous AND Aging > SLA

2011.name: Ownerless Non-PO Invoice
2011.mentalModel: A non-PO invoice arrives with no PO and no requester named on it. Left alone it ages in the unassigned queue for weeks. I infer the likely owner from GL history, the vendor, and the contract, and route it — an inferred owner who can reassign beats a silent backlog.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2011.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Ownerless Non-PO Invoice pattern.
2011.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2011.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Missing_Owner
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Missing_Owner'

2012.name: OCR Confidence Cliff
2012.mentalModel: The capture engine is only 62% sure the invoice total is $12,400 — the tax and vendor fields are shaky too. Above the confidence bar I let it post untouched; below it, this is a keying error waiting to hit the ledger. I gate straight-through on confidence, not on hope.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

2012.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the OCR Confidence Cliff pattern.
2012.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
2012.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for OCR_Confidence
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'OCR_Confidence'

3001.name: Stuck Reconciliation Item
3001.mentalModel: This GL-to-subledger break has rolled forward three closes. Is it a timing item that will clear, or a real difference someone must own? Age it and route.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

3001.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Stuck Reconciliation Item pattern.
3001.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
3001.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Aging_Recon_Break
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Aging_Recon_Break'

3004.name: Bank Reconciliation Outlier
3004.mentalModel: A bank line has sat unmatched for two weeks — not an in-transit item, it's stopped clearing. Either a payment failed silently or something posted twice. I chase the outlier now, before it's buried in next month's noise.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

3004.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Bank Reconciliation Outlier pattern.
3004.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
3004.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Bank_Recon_Outlier
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Bank_Recon_Outlier'

3005.name: Suspense Account Creep
3005.mentalModel: The suspense and clearing accounts are quietly filling up — items land there 'temporarily' and never leave. A growing suspense balance is unreconciled risk in disguise; I age it and force each item to a home.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

3005.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Suspense Account Creep pattern.
3005.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
3005.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Suspense_Aging
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Suspense_Aging'

3006.name: Auto-Certified Reconciliation
3006.mentalModel: This account was certified 'reconciled' in four seconds flat, same as every month — but the supporting balance hasn't moved and nobody attached evidence. A rubber-stamped rec isn't a control; I re-open the risky ones for a real look.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

3006.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Auto-Certified Reconciliation pattern.
3006.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
3006.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for RubberStamp_Recon
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'RubberStamp_Recon'

3002.name: Late / Missing Accrual
3002.mentalModel: A recurring accrual that posted every month is missing this period. Did the activity stop, or did someone forget? Detect the gap before the books close.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

3002.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Late / Missing Accrual pattern.
3002.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
3002.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Accrual_Gap
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Accrual_Gap'

3003.name: Intercompany Out-of-Balance
3003.mentalModel: Entity A booked the charge; Entity B never picked it up. Every day this lingers it distorts the consolidation. Match the legs and chase the missing one.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

3003.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Intercompany Out-of-Balance pattern.
3003.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
3003.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for IC_Imbalance
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'IC_Imbalance'

3007.name: Close Task Critical-Path Slip
3007.mentalModel: A close task just slipped and it's on the critical path — every downstream task is now waiting on it. I don't need a status meeting; I need to know who the one bottleneck is and nudge them before the whole close moves a day right.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

3007.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Close Task Critical-Path Slip pattern.
3007.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
3007.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Close_CriticalPath
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Close_CriticalPath'

3008.name: Period Cut-Off Error
3008.mentalModel: A big invoice for in-period services posted two days after the cut-off, into the next month. Booked in the wrong period it distorts both months' results. I catch cut-off errors at close, not when the auditor does.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

3008.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Period Cut-Off Error pattern.
3008.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
3008.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for CutOff_Error
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'CutOff_Error'

4001.name: Stalled Onboarding Provisioning
4001.mentalModel: Day-one is in 48 hours and laptop, accounts, and badge are still 'pending'. Which task is the bottleneck, and who do I nudge before the new hire shows up to nothing?

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

4001.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Stalled Onboarding Provisioning pattern.
4001.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
4001.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Provisioning_Stall
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Provisioning_Stall'

4003.name: Manager Onboarding No-Show
4003.mentalModel: Day-one is 48 hours out and the hiring manager still hasn't done a single onboarding task — no buddy, no equipment sign-off, no first-week plan. The new hire will show up to a manager who forgot they were coming. I nudge the manager before the impression is set.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

4003.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Manager Onboarding No-Show pattern.
4003.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
4003.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Manager_Prep_Gap
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Manager_Prep_Gap'

4004.name: Access Not Ready Day-1
4004.mentalModel: Laptop's here, but the accounts — email, VPN, the core app — still aren't provisioned and start is tomorrow. A new hire who can't log in on day one loses their first week and their first impression. I chase the access owner, not just the asset.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

4004.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Access Not Ready Day-1 pattern.
4004.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
4004.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Access_Provisioning_Gap
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Access_Provisioning_Gap'

4005.name: Ghost-Start Risk
4005.mentalModel: The new hire has gone quiet — didn't confirm start details, didn't open the pre-boarding portal, and it's a hot market. Ghosting after accept is real, and every provisioned laptop and licence is sunk cost if they no-show. I re-engage now and warn the manager to keep a backup warm.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

4005.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Ghost-Start Risk pattern.
4005.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
4005.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for NoShow_Signal
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'NoShow_Signal'

4002.name: Missing Compliance Document
4002.mentalModel: Right-to-work paperwork still isn't on file and the start date is locked. Chase it now, or we have a compliance exposure on day one.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

4002.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Missing Compliance Document pattern.
4002.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
4002.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Doc_Gap
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Doc_Gap'

4006.name: Background-Check Stall
4006.mentalModel: The background check has been 'in progress' for two weeks and the start date is locked. Let them start before it clears and we've got an unvetted person in a sensitive role; hold it blindly and we lose the hire. I chase the vendor and prep the conditional-start call.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

4006.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Background-Check Stall pattern.
4006.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
4006.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for BGCheck_Stall
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'BGCheck_Stall'

4007.name: Expiring Work Authorization
4007.mentalModel: This hire's work permit expires four months after they start — right in the middle of probation. An expiring authorization nobody's tracking becomes an illegal-to-employ problem overnight. I flag the renewal runway now, while there's still time to act.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

4007.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Expiring Work Authorization pattern.
4007.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
4007.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for WorkAuth_Expiry
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'WorkAuth_Expiry'

4008.name: Certification / License Gap
4008.mentalModel: This role legally requires a professional licence, and the one on file can't be verified against the register — maybe lapsed, maybe never held. Putting an unlicensed person in a regulated seat is a fine and a headline. I verify at source before day one.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

4008.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Certification / License Gap pattern.
4008.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
4008.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for License_Gap
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'License_Gap'

5001.name: Stockout Risk Signal
5001.mentalModel: Demand is trending up and on-hand cover just dropped below lead time. Do I expedite, substitute, or ride it out? Decide before the shelf goes empty.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

5001.conceptualSQL:

5002.name: Delivery Slippage
5002.mentalModel: The carrier's ETA just slipped past the customer's promised date. Do I re-route, split the shipment, or proactively tell the customer? Don't let them find out first.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

6000.name: Budget Sandbagging
6000.mentalModel: The regional budget landed 15% below current run-rate — right after their bonus was tied to budget attainment. That's not conservatism, it's a sandbag: lowball the plan so any outcome looks like a beat.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

6000.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Budget Sandbagging pattern.
6000.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
6000.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Sandbag_Signal
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Sandbag_Signal'

6001.name: Hockey-Stick Forecast
6001.mentalModel: Every quarter the forecast crawls flat then miraculously spikes in the final month to hit the number. The back-loaded hockey stick almost never lands — I discount the miracle month before it's baked in.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

6001.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Hockey-Stick Forecast pattern.
6001.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
6001.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for BackLoaded_Curve
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'BackLoaded_Curve'

6002.name: Forecast Drift
6002.mentalModel: The forecast still assumes 8% growth three months after the market turned. Nobody refreshed the driver — the gap between what we're steering by and reality is widening silently.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

6002.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Forecast Drift pattern.
6002.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
6002.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Assumption_Staleness
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Assumption_Staleness'

6003.name: Reforecast Trigger Miss
6003.mentalModel: A material event just hit — a lost contract, an FX swing — but no reforecast was triggered. The number the business is steering by is already wrong and nobody's noticed.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

6003.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Reforecast Trigger Miss pattern.
6003.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
6003.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Reforecast_Trigger
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Reforecast_Trigger'

6004.name: Unexplained Variance
6004.mentalModel: A $2M unfavourable variance and the commentary just says 'timing'. Timing three months running isn't timing — it's a miss nobody wants to name. I want the real driver, not a placeholder word.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

6004.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Unexplained Variance pattern.
6004.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
6004.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Variance_Unexplained
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Variance_Unexplained'

6005.name: Offsetting Variance Masking
6005.mentalModel: Net variance is near zero, so it looks clean — but it's a big favourable price hiding an equally big unfavourable volume. The net tells a comforting lie; the components tell the real story.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

6005.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Offsetting Variance Masking pattern.
6005.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
6005.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Offsetting_Variance
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Offsetting_Variance'

6006.name: Phasing / Timing Distortion
6006.mentalModel: The whole variance is phasing — spend that shifted a month, not real overspend. Raising an alarm on a calendar artefact wastes everyone's time; I reclass it to the right period and move on.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

6006.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Phasing / Timing Distortion pattern.
6006.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
6006.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Phasing_Artifact
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Phasing_Artifact'

6007.name: Allocation Dispute
6007.mentalModel: The cost centre is up in arms about a variance driven entirely by a corporate allocation they don't control. Before anyone's judged on it, I separate what they own from what was pushed down to them.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

6007.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Allocation Dispute pattern.
6007.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
6007.conceptualSQL:
-- sample trigger for Allocation_Split
SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Allocation_Split'

7000.name: Idle Balance Sweep Miss
7000.mentalModel: €4M sat in a non-interest current account over the weekend while another entity drew on an overdraft. We paid to borrow what we already had — sweep the idle balance before it costs us on both sides.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

7000.layer2_eventSeries: Sample event series derived from the source timeline.
7000.conceptualSQL:

SELECT * FROM source WHERE signal = 'Idle_Balance'

7001.name: Cash-Forecast Variance
7001.mentalModel: Actual cash came in €3M under the 5-day forecast — a major customer paid late and nobody flagged it. If the forecast is this wrong we could walk into a funding gap blind.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)

7001.layer1_logicalMapping: Sample logical mapping for the Cash-Forecast Variance pattern.

7002.name: Trapped Cash
7002.mentalModel: We keep counting the Brazil balance as available, but repatriation rules mean we can't actually move it without a haircut. Trapped cash on the dashboard is a liquidity illusion — I ring-fence it.

sap: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
oracle: (sample mapping — connect your system of record)
